text: The exercise demonstrates an anterior needle drive vertical technique, simulating suturing on the anterior abdominal wall. Two rows of parallel dots are visible, with suturing performed from top to bottom to simulate closing a vertical defect. The needle enters at the lateral dot and exits at the medial dot, illustrating the lateral to medial orientation of suture throws.

The surgeon demonstrates a right-sided suture throw, loading the needle to pronate the hand through the throw. The needle is pulled out following its curve to minimize tissue stress. On the left side, the needle is loaded in the left hand, requiring pronation for the throw, with the right hand pulling the needle through, enhancing range of motion and efficiency.

Staying efficient with your movements and moving quickly will allow you to achieve a high score. However, the exercise is very critical of any excessive force. So that really has to be taken into account in order to try and achieve the highest score.
